content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · · Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5991          322-6441          322-6444


January 9, 1979


Anthony C. Bennetti
Deputy City Attorney
151 West Mission Street
San Jose, CA 95110

Dear Mr. Bennetti:

     This is in response to your letter of December 22, 1978
seeking informal advice concerning the Political Reform Act
on behalf of Councilmember James Self.  I hope the following
advice provided pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b)
is helpful.

     The facts, as I understand them from you letter and from
a subsequent conversation with Mr. Logan, are as follows.  As
a member of the City Council, Councilmember Self is also a
member of the Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Jose.
He is currently in a position to obtain a finder's fee of
several thousand dollars for bringing together the owner of a
commercial building, located on Santa Clara Street between
Market and First Streets, and a prospective purchaser who
wants to renovate the building.  This building is across
Santa Clara Street to the north from the Pueblo Uno Redevelop-
ment Project and is two blocks north of the San Antonio Plaza
Redevelopment Project.  It is a Victorian building of two or
three stories with a narrow frontage.  The ground floor is
adapted for general retail use.  This building, like most of
the other buildings in the surrounding core area whether inside
or outside of the established redevelopment projects, needs
extensive renovation and has declined in value over the last
ten to twenty years.

     You have asked whether the payment of the finder's fee to
Councilmember Self will require him to disqualify himself from
decisions which serve to implement either the Pueblo Uno Project

Mr. Anthony C. Bennetti                                     Page Two
January 9, 1979


or the San Antonio Plaza Project. You have also asked whether
our answer to the foregoing question depends on who actually
provides the fee to Councilmember Self.

     Section 87100 1/ of the Political Reform Act provides that:

          No public official at any level of state or
          local government shall make, participate in making
          or in any way attempt to use his official position
          to influence a governmental decision in which he
          knows or has reason to know he has a financial
          interest.

     Section 87103(c) provides that an official has a financial
interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on a source of income
of $250 or more received by or promised to the official within
twelve months prior to the time when the decision is made.

     2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(a)(3)(B), part of the
Commission's regulation defining "material financial effect,"
provides that a decision will have a material financial effect
on a source of income if there is a nexus between the governmental
decision and the purpose for which the official receives income.

     The stated objectives of the City Council, acting as the
Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Jose, in creating the
Pueblo Uno Redevelopment Area are to:

     1.  Promote rehabilitation and eliminate causes of physical
         and economic blight;

     2.  Support and enhance the values of private properties
         and improvements;

     3.  Create an attractive urban environment to bring people
         back into the core area; and

     4.  Attract additional private investment into the project
         and adjoining areas.

                                    Pueblo Uno Redevelopment Plan
                                    (May 1975) p. 4


_________________________________
1/   All statutory references are to the Government Code unless
     otherwise noted.

Mr. Anthony C. Bennetti                                    Page Three
January 9, 1979


     In addition, the Pueblo Uno Plan provides that "In the event
an owner, for any reason, is unable to participate in rehabilita-
tion, the Agency may assist that owner in finding a developer
for the property who will agree to rehabilitation of the property
in conformance with the Plan." Section 301, p. 13. Although the
focus of the San Antonio Plaza Project is on new construction
rather than renovation of existing structures, its general pur-
pose can also be described as the upgrading of real property in
order to stimulate the economy and to stem the decline of both
the condition and value of the buildings throughout the core
area. Any decision to implement either of these plas must be
assumed to be in furtherance of these objectives.

     Regardless of who ultimately provides Councilmember Self
with the finder's fee, if the proposed transaction were to
take place, he would be receiving income for putting the building
in the hands of someone who will renovate it and thereby further
the objectives of redevelopment in the core area in general.
There would therefore be a nexus between the purpose for which he
received the income and any decision he might thereafter make,
in his capacities as City Councilmember and member of the Re-
development Agency of the City of San Jose, to implement re-
development within the core area of the city.

     If Councilmember Self proceeds with the transaction in
question, he should therefore disqualify himself from partici-
pation in any official decision to implement either the Pueblo
Uno Redevelopment Project or the San Antonio Plaza Redevelopment
Project from the time at which the transaction becomes suffin-
ciently formalized so that the finder's fee in effect becomes
promised" to him until 12 months after he has actually received
it.

     If you have any questions concerning this advice, please
do not hesitate to call me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Sarah T. Cameron
                                        Counsel
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

STC:nrw

. . . . .

[SEAL: City of San Jose seal]     OFFICE OF

                                  CITY ATTORNEY
                                  CITY OF SAN JOSE


ROBERT J. LOGAN                                    151 WEST MISSION STREET
CITY ATTORNEY                                      SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95110
                                                   (408) 277-4454


                                                   December 22, 1978


     State of California
     Fair Political Practices Commission
     P.O. Box 807
     Sacramento, CA  95804

     Re:  Request for a Conflict of Interest Opinion

     Dear Gentlepersons:

          On behalf of the City Attorney's office of the City of
     San Jose, we request your opinion on the following situation:

          Councilmember James Self, sits as both a member of the
     City Council of the City of San Jose and as a Director of the
     Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Jose.

          Councilmember Self is aware of certain commercial property
     in the downtown of the City of San Jose which the owner desires
     to sell.  This property is improved with commercial buildings in
     need of some rehabilitation.  The property is situated approximately
     one block from the San Antonio redevelopment project area and approx-
     imately and approximately two blocks from the Pueblo Uno redevelopment
     project area.

          Councilmember Self is also aware of an interested purchaser for
     the subject commercial property.

          Councilmember Self proposes to act as a "finder" in getting the
     interested seller and the interested purchaser together.  If a sales
     transaction results, Councilmember Self will realize a finder's fee
     amounting to several thousand dollars.  He will have no ownership
     interest or other participation interest in the property.  The only
     other service which might be provided will be to recommend to the
     buyer certain building contractors who would be available for rehabil-
     itation of the buildings.  There are no conditions attached to the

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Request for a Conflict of Interest Opinion
Councilmember Self

Page 2
12/22/78

transaction which are related to the official duties of Councilmember Self, either as a Councilperson or as a Director of the
Redevelopment Agency.

Given the foregoing, the question arises:

1. Will Councilmember Self be required to disqualify
himself from participating in decisions of the Redevelopment
Agency or the City with respect to implementation of the
San Antonio redevelopment project or the Pueblo Uno
redevelopment project, assuming that the implementation
of such plans will be to improve the ultimate fair market
value of the commercial property at some time in the future.
This question also assumes that none of the activities of
the Redevelopment Agency or of the City will affect the fair
market value of the property in the transaction between the
interested buyer and the interested seller nor will the
activities of the Agency or the City be involved with the
amount of Councilmember Self's finder's fee?

2. Does it make a difference if the finder's fee
accrues to Councilmember Self a) from the seller, b) from the
buyer, or c) from a real estate agent who may later become
involved in the transaction and the negotiations between
buyer and seller?

We appreciate your earliest response to this request, and if
you have any request for further information, please do not hesitate
to contact Deputy City Attorney Anthony C. Benetti at (408) 277-
4458.

Very truly yours,

ROBERT J. LOGAN
City Attorney

By: [signature]
ANTHONY C. BENETTI
Deputy City Attorney

ACB:lm

cc: Councilmember J. Self

sections.question: 1. Will Councilmember Self be required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions of the Redevelopment Agency or the City with respect to implementation of the San Antonio redevelopment project or the Pueblo Uno redevelopment project, assuming that the implementation of such plans will be to improve the ultimate fair market value of the commercial property at some time in the future. This question also assumes that none of the activities of the Redevelopment Agency or of the City will affect the fair market value of the property in the transaction between the interested buyer and the interested seller nor will the activities of the Agency or the City be involved with the amount of Councilmember Self's finder's fee? 2. Does it make a difference if the finder's fee accrues to Councilmember Self a) from the seller, b) from the buyer, or c) from a real estate agent who may later become involved in the transaction and the negotiations between buyer and seller?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Councilmember who also serves as a Redevelopment Agency Director must recuse himself from participating in redevelopment decisions when he stands to earn a finder's fee from facilitating a commercial property sale near those project areas, and whether the source of the finder's fee affects this conflict analysis.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter (not an FPPC response). Document dated 12/22/78, filed as 79-112. No FPPC conclusion or response is included in this document. Two specific questions are clearly articulated regarding conflict of interest and recusal requirements.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of San Jose requests an FPPC conflict of interest opinion regarding Councilmember James Self's proposed role as a 'finder' in a commercial property transaction while also serving as a City Council member and Redevelopment Agency Director, and whether he must recuse himself from redevelopment decisions that could indirectly benefit the property.
